findings.message: Plan claims 'plugin count 51 to 52 in four locations' and the risks table references line 3, 7, 145, 147 of root README. Actual README has literal '51' at lines 3, 7, 45, 159, 161, 356 — six occurrences, not four, and the cited line numbers 145/147 are wrong (the collection summary is at 159/161). Also the backticked plugin list at line 163 must get a new plugin-dev entry inserted alphabetically — not mentioned at all. Validator only enforces 3 patterns (expert plugins, **N** plugins, SkillStack collection summary) so prose mentions at lines 45 and 356 will silently drift.
findings.suggestion: Rewrite Task 1 and Task 15 to enumerate exact line numbers: 3, 7, 45, 159, 161, 356. Also bump the backticked plugin list at line 163 and the Development category header at line 226 from (11) to (12). Add DoD: `grep -n '\b51\b' README.md` returns zero hits after Task 15.

findings.message: Repo baseline has an untracked `elicitation/` plugin directory (git status shows `?? elicitation/`). The root README already counts it in '51 plugins' and the filesystem validator currently reports 51 plugins passing. If the implementer works in a clean worktree without elicitation committed, the count math breaks. The plan does not acknowledge this pre-existing partial state.
findings.suggestion: Add a Task 0 preflight: verify `git status` shows elicitation committed (or commit it) before starting Task 1. Add an assumption check: 'baseline validator run reports 51 plugins with zero errors on current main'. Document in Assumptions.

findings.area: Task 1 / catalog version-equality not in DoD
findings.message: Task 1 creates plugin.json AND updates registry.json AND marketplace.json atomically, but the DoD does not require version string equality across all three. validate_catalog (validate_plugins.py line 293) errors on any drift. A typo like '1.0.0' vs '1.0' would cause a validator fail that the DoD checklist does not catch.
findings.suggestion: Add DoD checkbox: plugin.json version, registry entry version, marketplace entry version are byte-for-byte identical. Add verify step to diff the three values.

findings.area: Task 4 / run_eval.py offline mode is tautological
findings.message: Task 4 offline-mode trigger evaluation uses 'a deterministic stub that checks whether the skill description contains any of the query keywords as a proxy for real activation'. This is tautological: a skill with the query keyword in its description always passes; one without always fails. The output is structurally valid but semantically meaningless. Task 9 DoD requires the offline run to 'complete without errors' — which it will, but users reading the benchmark report will trust a meaningless number.
findings.suggestion: Offline mode must emit a loud banner: 'OFFLINE MODE: results are SYNTHETIC, do not reflect real activation behavior'. Add plugin-evaluation SKILL.md copy: '--offline is for CI smoke-testing only; real evals require an API key'. Add DoD: offline JSON output has fields `mode: offline` and `warning: synthetic results`.

findings.message: Risks table rates validator drift as LOW likelihood, LOW impact. The repo already has TWO validators: .github/scripts/validate_plugins.py (plugin-level, 366 lines) and skill-creator/skills/skill-creator/scripts/validate_skill.py (skill-level, different contract). The plan adds a THIRD: plugin-dev/scripts/validate_plugin.py. Three validators with overlapping responsibility and no shared test suite is a maintenance trap. The commit-SHA reference in the header is documentary only — it won't prevent drift, only help forensics after drift happens.
findings.suggestion: Add a drift-detection contract test in test_validate_plugin.py: run both validators against skillstack-workflows and assert the same error output shape. Upgrade risk likelihood from LOW to MEDIUM in the risks table. Consider factoring shared helpers (parse_frontmatter, load_json) into a small importable module both validators use — violates duplicate-and-decouple but solves the real problem.

findings.area: Task 8 / hook reference budget 2x too small
findings.message: Task 8 budgets hook-event-reference.md at ~280 lines for 28 events with full JSON schema, matcher semantics, exit code semantics, and decision JSON shapes. That is 10 lines per event, which cannot cover even the schema alone. Combined with the no-invention rule (line 490), the implementer will either truncate content or exceed budget.
findings.suggestion: Raise budget to 20-30 lines per event (560-840 lines total). Or split: core 10 events inline in SKILL.md at 25 lines each, advanced 18 events in hook-event-reference.md at 15 lines each (~270 lines plus headers). Explicitly acknowledge which events get full-schema treatment and which get reference-only.

findings.message: The only cited authoritative source is /tmp/anthropic-skills-guide.txt which explicitly does NOT mention hooks. The 28-event claim comes from a sub-agent research report that may be gone from /private/tmp/claude-503/ by the time Task 8 runs. No primary docs.claude.com fetch is planned.
findings.suggestion: Add Task 8 pre-step: fetch docs.claude.com/en/docs/claude-code/hooks via WebFetch and cross-check that the 28 events claimed in the research report match the live docs. Enumerate the 28 events in the plan itself (not just 'all 28 events from the research report') so drift is visible.

findings.message: Task 14 line 684 prescribes `pip install pytest pyyaml` and `pytest tests/ -v`. Repo-wide Python standard: 'Use uv for ALL Python operations. NEVER use pip directly.' The existing pytest-skill-creator job still uses pip (predating the standard). Task 14 perpetuates the old pattern.
findings.suggestion: Use `uv pip install pytest pyyaml` or `uv run pytest -q`. Also use `-q` instead of `-v` per the same standard. If parity with pytest-skill-creator is intentional, document the choice. Better: migrate all pytest jobs in the same PR.

findings.area: Task 6 / bash test silent skip on macOS
findings.message: Task 6 test_test_hook.py exercises test_hook.sh via subprocess. Task 6 DoD says 'timeout behavior (skip if gtimeout not available on macOS)'. Silent skip of a core assertion is not a real test. Developers on macOS without coreutils get a green pytest that never exercised the timeout path.
findings.suggestion: Install a PATH shim that fakes `timeout` for the test, or document `brew install coreutils` as a prerequisite and fail loudly if neither binary is present rather than skipping.

findings.area: Task 4 vs Task 9 circular verify path
findings.message: Task 9 verification runs run_eval.py (built in Task 4) against evals created in Task 9. If the eval file schema does not match what run_eval.py expects, the implementer gets a tight feedback loop with no fixture to anchor. Task 4 verify-step (line 358) hints at this but leaves it informal.
findings.suggestion: Add Task 4 DoD: ship an inline fixture eval file (3 trigger queries, 1 output case) that locks down the expected schema. Task 9 must reuse that exact fixture as its shape anchor.

findings.message: PRD Key Decisions table says 'cloud-finops has 26'. Filesystem shows cloud-finops is a single-skill plugin at cloud-finops/skills/cloud-finops/ with references. The '26' number is wrong. Cosmetic but undermines the '7 is in range' argument.
findings.suggestion: Correct the PRD: 'skillstack-workflows has 9 skills; cloud-finops has 1 skill + 20 references; 7 multi-skills is in range for this repo'. Or drop the comparison.

findings.message: Task 3 claims idempotency ('same invocation with same args twice should produce same output') but no DoD test enforces it. A single datetime.now() in generated comments breaks determinism silently.
findings.suggestion: Add DoD: test_scaffold_is_deterministic runs the scaffolder twice into tmp_path and asserts byte-equal output. Forbid datetime.now() / os.urandom() / uuid.uuid4() in scaffold_plugin.py.

findings.message: SKILL.md and reference line caps are enforced, but no task DoD requires every factual claim to cite either a live URL or a specific research report path. Without this, the anti-fabrication guidance in Task 8 (line 490) does not propagate to the other content tasks.
findings.suggestion: Lift the 'does not invent' phrasing from Task 8 into a shared content-authoring DoD applied to Tasks 7-13.

findings.message: Default timeout is 5 seconds. Real hooks (TypeScript formatters, linters) often exceed 5s under CI load. The plan does not make the default configurable via env.
findings.suggestion: Raise default to 15s and allow override via TEST_HOOK_TIMEOUT env var. Document that 5s was for trivial smoke tests only.

findings.message: Bottom-up rationale holds for scripts-vs-skills that depend on scripts. But Tasks 10 (architecture), 12 (ideation), 13 (research) do not depend on any script. Building them late means the research report files in /private/tmp/claude-503/ have more time to be garbage-collected. Assumption at line 132 admits the fallback to PRD summaries is weak.
findings.suggestion: Move Tasks 12 and 13 to run immediately after Task 1. They provide a quick win and derisk the research-file-decay problem.

findings.message: Task 1 ships a skill with `description: Placeholder ... Will be populated in Task 7` and commits catalog entries. If anyone installs on the intermediate commit, they get 'Placeholder' text in the activation description. Violates the 'no promotional/placeholder text in descriptions' guideline.
findings.suggestion: Either write a minimal-but-real plugin-validation SKILL.md in Task 1 (real one-paragraph scope + trigger phrases), or hold catalog entries until Task 15. The former is easier; content enhancement in Task 7 still makes sense.

findings.message: Task 15 DoD says scenario table 'has at least 5 rows' but the PRD describes 4 core user flows. A 5th row would be fabricated.
findings.suggestion: Change DoD to 'at least 4 rows matching the PRD user flows'.

findings.message: PRD line 237: 'Plugin-shipped agents cannot include hooks, mcpServers, or permissionMode'. Task 8 hook-handler-types.md (180 lines for 4 handler types) does not have a DoD requirement to cover this security-critical restriction for agent handlers.
findings.suggestion: Add DoD to Task 8: hook-handler-types.md includes an explicit 'Security: restrictions on agent handlers' subsection citing the restriction.

findings.message: Task 14 sets continue-on-error: false. Existing pytest-docker and pytest-mcp use continue-on-error: true (soft-fail). Hard-fail is more rigorous but blocks PRs on any offline-mode flakiness.
findings.suggestion: Ship the first version with continue-on-error: true, flip to false after one week of green runs. Comment the temporary policy in the job YAML.

findings.area: Missing risk: alphabetic insertion across 3 catalog files
findings.message: Plan correctly identifies that plugin-dev goes between persona-mapping and prioritization in registry.json (development category). But registry.json, marketplace.json, root README line 163 backticked list, and the README Development section table all have their own orderings. Easy to botch.
findings.suggestion: Add risk row: 'alphabetic insertion mismatch across registry.json / marketplace.json / README.md line 163 / README.md Development section'. Mitigation: diff-review all four insertions in one commit.

findings.message: The PRD is one of the better ones in this repo: problem-first framing, 4 concrete user flows, explicit in/out-of-scope with rationale, deep research findings with citations, and a named decision log. The plan inherits this structure and carries the decision rationale forward. Most plans would warrant rejection for missing half of this.

Task 1 anticipates the 'catalog-entries-must-land-in-same-commit-as-plugin-directory' trap (line 110, risks table row 1). This is a real gotcha that bites most plugin authors. The plan catches it upfront.
Task 3 has scaffold_plugin.py run validate_plugin.py on its own output. Strong feedback loop that prevents shipping a scaffolder that generates broken plugins.
Task 9 ships evals for the plugin-evaluation skill itself — the skill is its own test subject. Elegant, dogfoods the format it teaches, and rare in this repo.
Task 5 line 374 already anticipates the macOS vs Linux timeout command issue with a command -v fallback. Reviewer question 11 is partially pre-answered. Credit where due.

summary: Verdict: NEEDS FIXES. The plan is substantively sound — it inherits a well-researched PRD, correctly identifies the multi-skill plugin precedent (skillstack-workflows), anticipates the catalog-atomic-update trap, and structures work sensibly bottom-up. The six must-fix issues cluster in Task 1 (README plugin-count math is wrong at the line-number level; untracked elicitation plugin in baseline state is unacknowledged; catalog version-equality not in DoD), Task 2-4 (three-way validator drift is underestimated; offline eval mode produces tautological results without a SYNTHETIC warning), and Task 8 (hook reference budget is 2x too small for 28 events). None of these are structural blockers — they are concrete fixes the planner can apply in one iteration. Recommended path: single revision pass addressing must_fix items, then implement.
